notes: Fuse box locations and contents for E65. Exact fuse number can vary by build date — always check the printed diagram on the fuse box lid first. This data is the typical config for a 2007 GCC build.

boxes.title: Glovebox fuse box (passenger side)
boxes.location: Behind the glovebox. Open glovebox, push sides inward to release stops, lower fully. Fuse panel on the right wall.
boxes.access: No tools — just pinch glovebox sides and lower.

fuses.circuit: Brake light switch / CAS module wake

boxes.title: Boot/trunk fuse box (right side)
boxes.location: Boot, right side wall. Lift the right trim panel — 3 plastic clips. Battery is also in the boot on E65. Note: the Logic 7 amplifier itself is on the LEFT rear corner of the trunk (different side from this fuse box).
boxes.access: Pull right trim panel off — no tools needed.

fuses.circuit: Top HiFi / Logic 7 amplifier — CHECK THIS FOR AUDIO FAULT
Top HiFi amp (some build dates) — alternate to F53
Rear comfort module / rear roof console

boxes.title: Engine bay power distribution (E-box)
boxes.location: Engine bay, near windshield, passenger side. Black box marked 'E-Box' (Electronics Box). Lift cover.
boxes.access: Lift cover with hand. Battery shutdown recommended before working.

common_faults_by_circuit.no_audio: Check boot fuse F53 (30A HiFi amp) FIRST. €1 fix if blown. See faults entry 'logic7_amp_dead' for next steps.
common_faults_by_circuit.no_start: Check engine bay F108 (DME power) and F109 (fuel pump relay). Also F27 (CAS wake circuit).
common_faults_by_circuit.no_blower: Check glovebox F38 (30A blower motor). If blown again immediately = motor seized.
common_faults_by_circuit.speedometer_dropout: Not a fuse issue. DSC module fault — see faults DTC 5DF0-5DF3 wheel speed sensors.
common_faults_by_circuit.no_rear_seat_heating: Check boot fuse box rear heater circuit — varies by spec.
common_faults_by_circuit.interior_lights_dead: Glovebox F54 area + check FRM module on K-CAN (not OBD reachable).
common_faults_by_circuit.trunk_wont_open: F54 (trunk light/lid module). Also check trunk actuator + microswitch.
